diagnostic_interpretation.root_cause_reading: The default API stack is intentionally no-socket, so the API process cannot list Docker runtime containers from inside the container. Admin Runtime therefore reports sandbox container observation as degraded/unavailable even though the deployed sandbox provider is docker.
diagnostic_interpretation.fail_closed_policy: B3 capacity readiness must continue treating sandbox container observation as missing evidence while list_runtime_containers_status is unavailable.
diagnostic_interpretation.approved_fix_direction_required: Use controlled host-side observation or an explicitly approved sandbox compose path when operator evidence requires runtime container listing; do not loosen readiness and do not mount the Docker socket in the default stack by accident.

notes: API container Docker socket absent; default stack must stay no-socket.
The default compose does not mount Docker socket; docker-compose.sandbox.yml is the explicit socket-bearing path for approved sandbox-mode operation.
The overview sandbox remains degraded/unavailable for container observation, so this diagnostic stays fail-closed for B3.
This is diagnostic only and does not close B3/G7.
required_next_steps: Record all seven operator-reviewed B3 load-test gate packets before any B3 closure or production default increase.
Record b3_10x4_sdk_subagents profile evidence before any B3 closure.
Resolve sandbox observation through controlled host-side observation or explicit approved sandbox compose path before treating sandbox runtime-container visibility as B3 evidence.
Complete operator status-upgrade review before claiming G7 closure or 211 verified status.
